title: refactor: simplify registry pricing
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Replaces dated `cost` / `price` arrays with one `cost` rate object plus `priceMultiplier`.
- Defaults Pollen price multiplier to 1.5x for `paidOnly` models and 1x for free models, with overrides where needed to preserve historical effective prices.
- Clarifies units: `cost` / `totalCost` are provider USD-equivalent; `price` / `totalPrice` are customer Pollen charges.
- Keeps derived rates and provider costs unrounded in production; only the final Pollen charge keeps the current 8-decimal ledger precision.
- Normalizes float noise in the effective-price snapshot for review readability.
- Rounds displayed per-1M model prices to nearest cent instead of always ceiling them.

Intentional price changes:
- `gemini-fast` `promptAudioTokens`: $0.3 -> $0.9 per 1M tokens.
- `scribe`: $0.33 -> $0.22 per hour.

Renamed functions:
- `getActiveCostDefinition` -> `getCostDefinition`
- `getActivePriceDefinition` -> `getPriceDefinition`

Safety net:
- Adds an effective `{ cost, price }` snapshot for every model so future rate changes are explicit in PR diffs.

Verification:
- Biome clean for touched files.
- `tsc -p enter.pollinations.ai --noEmit` clean.
- `tsc -p gen.pollinations.ai --noEmit` clean.
- Snapshot consistency checked against the current registry.
- Local Vitest rerun is blocked in this Codex runtime by the Rollup native optional dependency code-signature issue; CI should run the full suite.

gist.headline: The ledger gets a polish
gist.blurb: We've refactored how model pricing is calculated under the hood, making the math cleaner and adding snapshot tests to keep future changes explicit.
gist.summary: Refactored the pricing registry to use a single cost rate object with a price multiplier, clarifying USD vs Pollen units and adjusting prices for gemini-fast audio ($0.3 to $0.9/1M tokens) and scribe ($0.33 to $0.22/hr).
gist.impact: Users will see cleaner, rounded per-1M token prices in the UI, while the underlying billing logic is now more robust and easier to maintain.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as an accountant, using a retro calculator or ledger book. Nomnom creature eating old complicated receipts. Setting: a cozy garden office.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing tiny reading glasses, using a chunky retro calculator on a wooden desk. The round Nomnom creature sits nearby, happily eating a stack of complicated paper receipts. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm cream gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
